message.content:
<deliberation>
Approach:
- Parsed each main GDPR node and identified 1-hop cross-references from the provided referenced_nodes.
- Reviewed the entire policy chunk against the combined requirements of the main node and its cross-references.
- Applied the instruction on omissions: missing topics in the policy chunk are treated as Non-Deviation.

Findings summary by node:
- REG-087 (Art49(2)) with reference to REG-086: The policy chunk does not address public register transfers; no contradictions detected.
- REG-029 (Art18(2)) with reference to REG-028: No mention of restriction of processing; no contradictions.
- REG-037 (Art21(3)): No mention of direct marketing or the right to object; no contradictions.
- REG-016 (Art14(1)) with references to REG-071, REG-078–REG-085, REG-086: The chunk does not discuss Art 14 disclosures; omissions treated as Non-Deviation.
- REG-012 (Art13(1)) with references to REG-071, REG-078–REG-085, REG-086: The chunk does not provide Art 13(1) disclosures; omissions treated as Non-Deviation.
- REG-010 (Art9(3)) with references to REG-009 and REG-008: No special category data or health/social care processing mentioned; no contradictions.
- REG-001 (Art6(1)): The chunk’s statements about contractual necessity align conceptually with Art6(1)(b) and do not negate other legal bases; no contradictions.
- REG-086 (Art49(1)) with references to REG-071, REG-078–REG-085: No mention of third-country transfers; no contradictions.

Conclusion: No deviations identified in the provided policy chunk for the evaluated nodes.
</deliberation>
